nuggets.headline: Kendrick Lamar and producer Mike WiLL Made-It had a years-long creative connection before *DAMN.*
nuggets.text: The urgent sound of *DAMN.*, particularly on tracks like "HUMBLE." and "DNA.", came from a long-standing creative relationship. Producer Mike WiLL Made-It had been working with Kendrick Lamar for years, exchanging beats since *good kid, m.A.A.d city*, but the timing for their collaboration to truly gel didn't strike until *DAMN.*. Their initial connection was facilitated by fellow TDE artist ScHoolboy Q, who encouraged Kendrick Lamar to link up with Mike WiLL Made-It in Atlanta.

source.title: How Mike WiLL Made-It And Kendrick Lamar Created The Year's Most Urgent Music : The Record : NPR
source.quoteSnippet: Me and [K.] Dot been working on this for years, ever since good kid, m.A.A.d city. Schoolboy Q is the one who really hooked us up.

nuggets.imageCaption: Kendrick Lamar in the studio, meticulously crafting the sound of his albums.

nuggets.headline: Kendrick Lamar's "HUMBLE." was nearly a track on Mike WiLL Made-It's *Ransom 2*.
nuggets.text: This powerful hit was one of the first tracks Kendrick Lamar created for *DAMN.*, but it almost had a different home. Producer Mike WiLL Made-It revealed that "HUMBLE." nearly wound up on his own album, *Ransom 2*, before Kendrick Lamar decided it was essential for *DAMN.*. The song’s direct and impactful energy quickly established it as a cornerstone of the album's sound and lyrical themes.

source.title: How Mike WiLL Made-It And Kendrick Lamar Created The Year's Most Urgent Music : The Record : NPR
source.quoteSnippet: Lamar's highest charting single to date, 'HUMBLE.,' almost wound up on Will's album Ransom 2 instead of K.Dot's

nuggets.imageCaption: The iconic album cover for Kendrick Lamar’s critically acclaimed *DAMN.*

nuggets.headline: Kendrick Lamar’s cousin Baby Keem used a beat from *DAMN.* sessions.
nuggets.text: If you're looking for another artist with a deep connection to Kendrick Lamar's unique vision, explore Baby Keem. Not only is he Kendrick Lamar's cousin, but a beat originally intended for *DAMN.* found its way to Keem's 2019 project *Die for My Bitch*. Kendrick Lamar himself praised Keem's rendition, stating he preferred what Keem did with the beat over his own original ideas for it. Start with *Die for My Bitch* to hear this fascinating creative crossover.

source.title: Kendrick Lamar sheds light on new album in interview with Baby ...
source.quoteSnippet: a DJ Dahi beat intended for Kendrick’s DAMN. ended up on Keem’s 2019 project Die for My Bitch: Kendrick: That was my favourite beat, I just forgot about it because that was a beat that I was supposed to put on Damn, but I never got to it.

nuggets.imageCaption: Kendrick Lamar with his cousin and collaborator, Baby Keem.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar Duckworth, from Compton, California, is critically acclaimed as one of the 21st century's most lauded artists. His work is known for lyrical mastery, conceptual vision, and storytelling that powerfully blends personal narratives with profound social commentary.
